name: Les Cordeliers
summary: Historic cloister in Saint-Emilion with sparkling wine production, visits, shop, bar, and courtyard picnic potential.
whyItMatters: This is a visually memorable wine stop inside the village, easier than a château transfer and good for mixed-interest groups.
pros: Official site has ticketing, visits, boutique, bar/catering, and sparkling-wine production sections.
Official site lists address in the cloister at 2 bis rue de la Porte Brunet.
Official site lists opening hours for much of the week.
cons: Not a classic still-red Saint-Emilion château tasting.
Exact July 2026 visit slots should be confirmed on the ticketing page.

logistics.opening_hours: Official site displayed Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Sun 10:30-19:00; verify Friday/Saturday and July 2026 details.
logistics.reservation_needed: Recommended for cellar visits; official site has a ticketing link.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until lodging/activity plan is fixed.
logistics.transit: Central Saint-Emilion village; verify walking route because village streets are steep/cobbled.
logistics.parking: Unknown; verify village parking if driving.

timing.best_visit_window: Late morning or afternoon in the Saint-Emilion village day.
timing.race_day_risk: Low unless paired with a Bordeaux race-day transfer.

sources.title: Les Cordeliers official website
sources.note: Supports venue identity, ticketing/visits/bar/boutique sections, displayed opening hours, and address.

sources.title: Decanter - Bordeaux travel: Where to eat and drink like a local
sources.note: Supports planning value as an easy Saint-Emilion picnic/wine option within town.

open_questions: Confirm Friday/Saturday opening hours and cellar tour availability for the exact travel date.
Check accessibility for anyone uncomfortable with steep cobblestones.
